method_atom: a self-driven active unit that converts locally consumed free energy into persistent directed motion, exhibiting a crossover from ballistic transport at short times to diffusive random-walk behavior at long times as orientational persistence decays, with an effective long-time diffusion coefficient enhanced above the passive thermal value and a short-time drift speed that scales with the local supply rate of the energy source.
object_atom: an artificial micro-scale catalytic colloidal swimmer that propels itself through a fluid via a chemical reaction catalyzed on its own surface, with propulsion driven by the surrounding concentration of fuel molecules.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the free-energy-to-motion conversion producing a ballistic-to-diffusive crossover with enhanced effective diffusion; the catalytic colloidal micro-swimmer consuming chemical fuel is the domain-specific object.
